title: Climate Change
shortDesc: Global temperatures have risen by 1.1°C since pre-industrial times...
description: Climate change refers to long-term shifts in global temperatures and weather patterns. Since the 1800s, human activities, especially burning fossil fuels, have been the main driver of climate change. Global average temperatures have risen by about 1.1°C above pre-industrial levels, causing more frequent extreme weather events, sea level rise, and threats to biodiversity.
facts: Global temperatures have risen by 1.1°C since pre-industrial times
The last decade (2011–2020) was the warmest on record
Arctic warming is happening twice as fast as the global average
Sea levels are rising at 3.3mm per year on average
actions: Reduce your carbon footprint by using public transport or cycling
Switch to renewable energy sources at home
Eat less meat and dairy products
Support and vote for climate-conscious policies

title: Deforestation
shortDesc: The world loses 10 million hectares of forest per year...
description: Deforestation is the large-scale removal of forests, primarily for agriculture, logging, and urban expansion. Forests are essential for absorbing CO₂, regulating the water cycle, and supporting biodiversity. The world is losing an estimated 10 million hectares of forest annually — an area larger than South Korea.
facts: 10 million hectares of forest are lost every year
The Amazon rainforest has lost about 17% of its cover in 50 years
Deforestation accounts for up to 10% of global carbon emissions
Over 80% of terrestrial species live in forests
actions: Choose FSC-certified wood and paper products
Reduce consumption of products linked to deforestation (palm oil, soy)
Support reforestation organizations
Advocate for stronger forest protection laws

title: Ocean Pollution
shortDesc: Over 8 million tonnes of plastic enter the oceans yearly...
description: Ocean pollution encompasses plastic waste, chemical runoff, oil spills, and noise pollution that harm marine ecosystems. More than 8 million tonnes of plastic enter the oceans every year. Microplastics have been found in the deepest ocean trenches and even in the human bloodstream.
facts: 8 million tonnes of plastic enter oceans every year
Over 1 million seabirds and 100,000 marine mammals die from ocean pollution annually
Microplastics have been detected in human blood and breast milk
The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is three times the size of France
actions: Use reusable bags, bottles and containers
Participate in beach and river clean-up events
Avoid single-use plastics whenever possible
Support policies that reduce industrial ocean dumping

title: Air Pollution
shortDesc: Air pollution kills approximately 7 million people per year...
description: Air pollution is contamination of the indoor or outdoor environment by any chemical, physical or biological agent that modifies the natural characteristics of the atmosphere. Around 99% of the global population breathes air that exceeds WHO guideline limits. Air pollution is the world's largest environmental health risk.
facts: Air pollution causes 7 million premature deaths each year
99% of the world's population breathes polluted air
Household air pollution from cooking affects 2.4 billion people
Air pollution costs the global economy over $5 trillion annually
actions: Use public transport, walk or cycle when possible
Avoid burning waste, wood or charcoal indoors
Support clean air regulations in your region
Use energy-efficient appliances at home

title: Biodiversity Loss
shortDesc: Species are disappearing 1,000 times faster than natural rates...
description: Biodiversity loss refers to the decline in the variety of living species on Earth. Current extinction rates are 100–1,000 times higher than natural background rates. Scientists warn that we are experiencing the sixth mass extinction event, driven by habitat destruction, climate change, pollution and overexploitation.
facts: 1 million plant and animal species face extinction
60% of wildlife populations have been wiped out since 1970
Only 23% of Earth's land remains in a wild state
Pollinators support 75% of global food crop production
actions: Plant native species in your garden
Avoid pesticides and herbicides
Support wildlife conservation organizations
Buy sustainably sourced food products

title: Water Scarcity
shortDesc: 2 billion people lack access to safe drinking water...
description: Water scarcity affects more than 40% of the global population and is projected to worsen with climate change. By 2025, two-thirds of the world could face water shortage conditions. Overuse in agriculture, industrial pollution and population growth are major drivers.
facts: 2 billion people lack safe drinking water at home
Agriculture uses 70% of the world's accessible freshwater
Water scarcity affects all continents
By 2050, up to 5.7 billion people could face water shortages
actions: Fix leaky faucets and reduce water waste at home
Collect and use rainwater for gardening
Choose water-efficient appliances
Support water conservation programs in your community
